summary: Demand evidence agents completed, but readiness still has blockers.

demand_status.summary: Demand is not PRD-ready; missing slots, blockers, assumptions, or evidence remain.

triage.explanation: Fast route by default; no hard factual risk trigger was found.

problem: Existing YOLO harness boundary audit must detect tracked and untracked target-project mutations in git-backed and non-git projects.
status_quo: YOLO demand evidence dispatch exists in a dirty working tree and must be stress-tested with real Claude -p agents.
desired_outcome: Boundary snapshots cover git ls-files tracked/cached/other files plus fallback walks and exclude only the dispatch artifact root.
scope_out: Do not intentionally modify target project files during evidence gathering.
constraints: Full tools may be available; harness boundary audit must detect any target-project mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Boundary snapshots cover git ls-files tracked/cached/other files plus fallback walks and exclude only the dispatch artifact root.
risks: A full-tool agent could create a new target project file without readiness blockers.

blockers.message: explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.

actions.reason: Verify blockers and assumptions are not promoted to facts.

protocol.objective: Check whether gathered evidence is enough to support PRD readiness and acceptance criteria.
protocol.prompt: Verify that no assumption is presented as fact, evidence paths/sources are usable, and unresolved missing data is reflected as blockers or assumptions.

agent_results.claim: Boundary snapshot implementation uses git ls-files for tracked/cached/other files and fallback walk for non-git projects, excludes allowed artifact roots from mutation detection, and is validated by tests for tracked, untracked, and non-git scenarios.

evidence.summary: gitFiles() runs 'git ls-files -co --exclude-standard -z' which lists tracked, cached, and other files while excluding standard patterns
evidence.why: Confirms git-backed file discovery covers all git file categories (tracked, cached, other)

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot() uses gitFiles() with fallback to walkFiles() and excludes allowedRoots
evidence.why: Shows the hybrid git/non-git strategy and artifact root exclusion logic

evidence.summary: walkFiles() recursively scans directories excluding common patterns (.git, node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, .cache)
evidence.why: Documents non-git fallback coverage and exclusion behavior

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots() compares before/after maps to detect added, modified, deleted files
evidence.why: Shows mutation detection logic that identifies all changes

evidence.summary: runDemandEvidenceDispatchRuntime() captures boundaryBefore/After snapshots, diffs them, and injects BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers
evidence.why: Confirms boundary audit integration in dispatch runtime

evidence.summary: Test 'boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass' writes unexpected-project-write.txt and expects blocked status with BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION
evidence.why: Verifies detection of tracked project file mutations

evidence.summary: Test 'git-backed boundary detects untracked project mutations' inits git repo, writes untracked-mutation.txt, expects violation with change path
evidence.why: Verifies detection of untracked file mutations in git-backed projects

evidence.summary: execution_policy.boundary sets project_mutation: 'forbidden' and allowed_write_roots to artifact output directory
evidence.why: Documents policy contract for boundary enforcement

risks: git ls-files -co may behave differently across git versions or platforms
walkFiles() fallback may miss platform-specific excluded patterns
Dirty working tree stress-testing with real Claude agents has not been verified in test evidence
missing: No test evidence for boundary behavior in truly non-git projects (walkFiles fallback)
No stress-test evidence using real Claude -p agents in dirty working trees
No test for edge cases: large file counts, binary files, permission errors during digest

result.notes: Core boundary audit implementation is verified for git-backed projects with tracked/untracked detection and artifact root exclusion. Missing test coverage for non-git fallback and real agent stress-testing are gap items but not blockers.

agent_results.claim: All 8 evidence citations from the explorer are accurate: paths, line numbers, and summaries match the actual code and tests. The core boundary snapshot implementation (git ls-files for tracked/cached/other files, walkFiles fallback, allowedRoots exclusion, mutation detection, and test validation) is verified by project-scoped evidence.

evidence.summary: VERIFIED: gitFiles() function at lines 62-69 uses 'git ls-files -co --exclude-standard -z' as claimed
evidence.why: Confirms explorer's git-backed file discovery evidence is accurate

evidence.summary: VERIFIED: buildBoundarySnapshot() at lines 97-108 uses gitFiles() with walkFiles() fallback and excludes allowedRoots as claimed
evidence.why: Confirms explorer's hybrid strategy and exclusion logic evidence is accurate

evidence.summary: VERIFIED: walkFiles() at lines 71-85 recursively scans directories as claimed
evidence.why: Confirms explorer's non-git fallback coverage evidence is accurate

evidence.summary: VERIFIED: diffBoundarySnapshots() at lines 110-120 compares before/after maps to detect changes as claimed
evidence.why: Confirms explorer's mutation detection logic evidence is accurate

evidence.summary: VERIFIED: runDemandEvidenceDispatchRuntime() at lines 511-567 captures before/after snapshots and injects BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers as claimed
evidence.why: Confirms explorer's boundary audit integration evidence is accurate

evidence.summary: VERIFIED: Test at lines 367-400 writes unexpected-project-write.txt and expects blocked status with BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION as claimed
evidence.why: Confirms explorer's tracked project file mutation test evidence is accurate

evidence.summary: VERIFIED: Test at lines 402-438 inits git repo, writes untracked-mutation.txt, expects violation with change path as claimed
evidence.why: Confirms explorer's untracked mutation test evidence is accurate

evidence.summary: VERIFIED: execution_policy.boundary at lines 243-246 sets project_mutation: 'forbidden' and allowed_write_roots as claimed
evidence.why: Confirms explorer's policy contract evidence is accurate

risks: git ls-files behavior may vary across git versions or platforms (not a blocker, noted by explorer)
walkFiles() fallback may miss platform-specific excluded patterns (not a blocker, noted by explorer)
Missing non-git fallback test coverage could allow regression (noted by explorer as gap)
Missing real agent stress-testing could miss production issues (noted by explorer as gap)

result.notes: All 8 explorer evidence citations are verified as accurate with correct paths, line numbers, and summaries. No assumptions promoted as facts. Missing items (non-git tests, stress testing) correctly identified in missing rather than claimed as facts. Core boundary audit implementation is sufficiently verified.
